programmes.show_description: Nightmare: Classic '70s police drama series. Could a nightmare had by Regan's girlfriend come true, is he really in imminent danger, and who is after him and why?
A Number of Old Wives' Tales: Drama series about a loveable rogue. Arthur gets involved with a bigamist who has several businesses run by women and who is married to all of them.

This programme brings you up to date with the latest action from the world of motorsport and features race coverage, insights, and behind-the-scenes footage from the best series.

All the racing, glamour and fun of Derby Day at Epsom Downs, including performances from Shergar and Sea The Stars and interviews with sports and entertainment personalities.
The latest news and reviews from the world of cars.
Brian Moore presents action from the 1984/85 football season including Ipswich Town vs Queens Park Rangers, Coventry City vs Newcastle United, and Everton vs Aston Villa.
Brian Moore presents action from the 1984/85 football season including Manchester United vs Tottenham Hotspur, Stoke City vs West Ham United, and Aston Villa v Norwich City.
All Thieves on Deck: Classic US action drama series. Thomas, TC and Rick are aboard a cruise ship and guarding a priceless wooden Hawaiian family god statue when it is stolen.
The Pride and the Princess: Classic US detective drama. Theo helps a Yugoslavian princess who has come to New York disguised as a nun to recover stolen family jewels from a mobster.
Two Men in a Vault: Classic drama. Ken and Harry are kept in the dark - and cause Rocky and Laura to become anxious - when they find themselves trapped in a vault over a weekend.
Feet of Clay: Action-packed police drama. Regan is contacted by a criminal from his past whose son has been kidnapped.
I Like Big Bots (That Will Not Die): US robot combat series. There is more carnage as 14 bots enter the arena, and legendary drum-spinner Yeti returns to the fray after an absence.
This Island Isn't Big Enough...: Classic US action drama series. Thomas, TC and Higgins search for Rick and a boatload of passengers who have gone missing at sea during a cruise.
Black Thorn: Classic US detective drama. Theo has another run-in with bounty hunter Salathiel Harris when he returns to New York in pursuit of a bail jumper.
Trial and Error: Classic drama. Ken tries to be a good Samaritan - but lands himself in trouble - when he tries to help a group of tenants being evicted by a ruthless landlord.
Newcastle: Leonna Mayor presents coverage of the fourth and final meeting of Friday Night Live from Newcastle. Commentary comes from Richard Hoiles.
Oscar-winning boxing drama film starring Sylvester Stallone and Talia Shire, 1976. A small-time boxer gets a chance at glory when he is challenged by a heavyweight champion.

Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling - the roster features world-class talent including Jon Moxley, Toni Storm, MJF, Ricochet and Adam Page.
The Ultra-Violence of Nick Gage: Wrestling documentary series. Nick Gage is the self-proclaimed king of deathmatch wrestling, but his road to the top includes prison and tragedies.
Phase One, Complete: American sitcom. Charlie becomes concerned when his new girlfriend Wendy bonds strongly with Jake, and Alan and Rose strike up a friendship through Scrabble.
Merry Thanksgiving: American sitcom. Charlie discovers that his favourite ex-girlfriend is getting married and tries desperately to convince her that he would make a better husband.

A look back at the wide variety of sports covered by ITV during the 1970s - from horse racing to table football and Formula One to darts - that includes many memorable moments.
Brian Moore presents action from April 1978 featuring West Ham United v Derby County, Nottingham Forest v Leeds United, and Swansea City v Brentford in the Fourth Division.
Join Brian Moore as Spurs come up against a stubborn Sunderland side in April 1978, West Ham travel up to the northwest to take on Manchester United, and Arsenal face Leeds United.
Money, Money, Money: Police drama series. Eddie Monk wins the pools, but then receives blackmail threats about the part he played in a robbery.
Oli Bell and the team are at Doncaster on the first day of the flat season.
Brian Moore introduces football highlights from November 1971, including an impressive Manchester City performance at Highbury against Arsenal.
Pilgrim of Hate: One last adventure for the medieval sleuthing monk. When the corpse of an old man is discovered in a sack at the Abbey, Cadfael believes that the man was murdered.
Melton Mowbray Pork Pies and Armitage Shanks Toilets: A look at the perfect pork pie, then how Britain's biggest maker of porcelain sanitaryware makes the nation's toilets.
Sharpe's Eagle: Napoleonic War-based action-adventure. Wellesley orders Sharpe to ensure that an arranged mission involving an incompetent colonel's new regiment is a success.

programmes.show_name: ITV Racing Live: Dubai World Cup
programmes.show_description: Live coverage of the 2026 Dubai World Cup from Meydan, a race which features an illustrious roll of honour, with some of the biggest names in flat racing having won the race.
A selection of the best hot shots from ITV Sport's snooker coverage.
Live coverage from the Gallagher PREM as Saracens take on Northampton Saints in The Showdown at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium.
A selection of goals from some of the superstars of the game - including Kevin Keegan and Paul Gascoigne - who played in the old First Division in the 1970s, 1980s and 1990s.
American Killers: Jeremy Wade travels to one of the largest dams in the Americas on the trail of a river monster that is responsible for the mysterious disappearance of corpses.
Action from the day's games in the Football League, including Huddersfield v Cardiff, Bradford v Plymouth, Northampton v Barnsley and Rotherham v Luton in League One.
Bond film starring Roger Moore and Lois Chiles, 1979. 007 blasts off to investigate when a spaceship is hijacked and comes face to face once more with metal-toothed Jaws.
